name: FluidType
description: Column fluid type

name: ModeCheck
description: Global check for mode change warnings while still in process mode

name: NStages
description: Column number of ideal stages excluding internal condenser and reboiler

name: FeedStage[S1]
description: Column feed stage locations

name: SideDraws
description:
Column side draw phase options.
     Liquid: enable liquid phase side draws. 
     Vapor: enable vapor phase side draws.
     Both: enable vapor and liquid phase side draws.

name: ReferenceStreams
description:
Column reference streams show options.
     Typical: Show condenser inlet, reboiler inlet, reflux, and reboiler boilup reference stream ports.
     Stages: Show stage net outgoing liquid or vapor reference stream ports.
     All: Show all reference stream ports.

name: Condenser
description:
Column condenser options.
     None: use for absorbers. 
     Internal: use for condensed columns in conceptual work.
          For a total condenser specify VFcond=0.
          For a partial condenser set VFcond>0.
          For a partial condenser at total reflux unspecify VFcond, specify Ldist=0 instead.
     External: use for detailed engineering when the condenser is modeled externally.

name: Reboiler
description:
Column reboiler options.
     None: Absorbers
     Internal: Reboiled columns for conceptual engineering projects.
     External: Separate flowsheet model for detailed engineering projects.
See Sump description for modeling kettle, or thermosyphon reboiler with baffle.

name: Sump
description:
Column sump options.
     None: No sump
     Initialize: Sump with an equation tear for easier solution of sump equations.
     Single: Sump full solution without a tear to make reboiler spec swaps.

     Internal kettle reboiler (PRO/II w/o baffle): Sump = None
     Internal thermosyphon reboiler (PRO/II w baffle): Sump = Single
     Any external reboiler: Sump = Single

name: Setup
description:
Column Setup options.
     Configure: initialize the column while configuring. 
     Solve: invoke the rigorous solution.

Before setting Setup to Solve:
     Set Sump = Initialize
     Set Efficiency = Contact, because the Contact mechanism involves homotopy-formulations that favor column convergence.

name: Capacity
description:
Column Capacity option. 
     Internals: apply the capacity correlations specific to the selected internal, thus correlating 
           the column's diameter Dcol to each stage's flooding factor FF, and the resulting 
           maximum flooding factor FFcol. 
     Ignore: ignore capacity correlations.
Open the Internals group to inspect further details for the hydraulic capacity calculations.

name: HydraulicsVapor
description:
Column hydraulic basis option for the thermophysical vapor phase properties. 
     Vapor from Stage: the properties of the vapor leaving the stage are used for the hydraulic calculations.
     Vapor to Stage: the properties of the vapor entering the stage from below are used for the hydraulic calculations. Vapor contributions of external feeds are combined with the vapor from below and the properties are reevaluated.
Open the Properties group to inspect the stagewise thermophysical properties.

name: HydraulicsLiquid
description:
Column hydraulic basis option for the thermophysical liquid phase properties. 
     Liquid from Stage: the properties of the liquid leaving the stage are used for the hydraulic calculations.
     Liquid to Stage: the properties of the liquid entering the stage from above are used for the hydraulic calculations. Liquid contributions of external feeds are combined with the liquid from above and the properties are reevaluated.
Open the Properties group to inspect the stagewise thermophysical properties.

name: StageProperties
description: Column stage properties option. \n     Required only: only the properties required for the selected hydraulic options are reported.\n     All: the properties of vapor and liquid to-stage and from-stage are reported.

name: PressureDrop
description:
Column pressure drop option.
     Constant: assign the specified DPStage to each stage.
     Profile: define a custom pressure drop profile using DPstep or by defining the DP for each stage.
     Internals: apply the pressure drop correlations based on the Internals model selected in the Sizing Group, thus deriving 
          each stage's pressure drop DP, and the resulting mean average value DPStage.
Open the Internals group to inspect further details for the hydraulic calculations.

name: InletPressures
description:
Column inlet pressure option.
     Ignore: allow any feed inlet pressure.
     Balanced: automatically set the feed inlet pressures equal to their respective feed stage pressures. Therefore, exactly 
     one pressure specification on the feed line must be freed up, typically a valve's, a pipe's or an exchanger's DP specification.
'Balanced' is required for Fluid Flow and Dynamic modes.

name: Basis
description:
Column reactive distillation basis corresponding to the selected conversion, equilibrium, or kinetic reaction submodel. 
     CatalystMass: use for kinetic reactions related to catalyst mass. 
     Volume: use for kinetic reactions related to catalyst volume.
     Conversion: use for conversion reactions.
     Equilibrium: use for equilibrium reactions, or when ColRxnSet is applied.

name: Balances
description: Column balance reporting option

name: Scheme
description:
Column control schemes that enable column control by means of three options.
See the model type help documentation, chapters 13 and 14, for more information. 

Control Ports: adds the following Control Ports that serve for PID inputs: 
 1. Port Cond_Level, port Reb_Level, 
 2. Port Top_Pressure, 
 3. Multi port Stage_Temperature and Control ports that serve for PID outputs, 
 4. Port CondHeat_Control, 
 5. Port RebHeat_Control, and 
 6. port Reflux_Control. 
These control ports allow a fully flexible control scheme configuration in combination
with the PID controller from the Controls library. 

Total Condenser: addressing total condensing operations,
adds internal product valves for the overhead vapor, the distillate and the bottoms
products as well as for the reflux, the condenser duty and the reboiler duty, combined
with these internal PID controllers: 
 1. TC1 controlling Stage[1].T by manipulating the reflux valve, 
 2. PC1 controlling Cond.P by manipulating the cooling duty valve, 
 3. LC1 controlling Cond.Level by manipulating the distillate valve, 
 4. TC2 controlling Stage[N].T by manipulating the heating duty valve, 
 5. LC2 controlling Reboiler.Level by manipulating the bottoms valve. 

Total Reflux: addressing total reflux/partial condensing operations,
adds internal product valves for the overhead vapor and the bottoms products, as well as for
the reflux, the condenser duty and the reboiler duty, combined with these internal PID controllers: 
 1. TC1 controlling Stage[1].T by manipulating the condenser cooling duty valve CondHeat_Control, 
 2. PC1 controlling Cond.P by manipulating the vapor product valve, 
 3. LC1 controlling Cond.Level by manipulating the reflux valve, 
 4. TC2 controlling Stage[N].T by manipulating the heating duty valve RebHeat_Control, 
 5. LC2 controlling Reboiler.Level by manipulating the bottoms valve.

name: Reactions
description: Column reactions option\nThis option relates to reactions that are specified in the Fluid, e.g. electrolytic reactions in the fluids Fluids.HCl_BE or Fluids.HCl_eNRTL.\nReactions specified in the 'Stage Reactions' group remain unaffected by this selection.

name: StaticHead
description:
Global static head option. 
     Include: include the static head contribution to the liquid product pressures of condenser, reboiler and sump.
     Ignore: ignore the static head contribution.
 The StaticHead option relates to Process mode only.

name: CompBasis
description:
Column basis for stage compositions and internal rate profiles.
     Molar: molar based profile variables.
     Mass: mass based profile variables. 
     Volumetric: actual volumetric based profile variables.
     MolarMass: molar and mass based profile variables. 
     All: molar, mass, and actual volumetric based profile variables.
When changing CompBasis, and non-default specifications are set on molar or mass based variables, e.g. V, it is best 
to use MolarMass or All to ensure all specifications remain listed.

name: Elevations
description:
Global selection to enable the specification of equipment port elevations.
     Disabled: disable specifications for port elevations.  The elevation change (DE) can still be specified in the Pipe and PipeRig models.
     Manual: manually specify port elevations. DE in the Pipe and PipeRig models can be swapped for port elevation specifications.
     Automatic: automatically calculate port elevations using equipment geometry and configuration options for select models (Column, Drum, etc).

When automatic elevations are enabled, an elevation specification must be removed from each inlet stream of impacted models (for example, remove the DE specification from an inlet Pipe).
Other model elevations must still be manually specified. For an explanation of how port elevations are estimated, see each model's help file.

name: Efficiency
description:
Column efficiency option. 
 
     Contact: Contact efficiency will be applied. 
          Contact efficiency represents an efficiency model based on the concept of 
          incomplete phase contact on stages. Contact efficiency also serves to initialize 
          the column solution based on the idea of phases that initially bypass each other 
          (when Contact is set to 0) before converging to a full solution (when Contact is 
          set to 1). 
          Efficiency should be set to Contact when configuring the column with 
          Setup=Configure, because the Contact mechanism involves homotopy-formulations 
          that favor column convergence.
          
      Initialize: Murphree vapor-phase efficiency will be initialized. 
          The overall Murphree vapor-phase efficiency and the stagewise efficiencies 
          for the specified Contact efficiency will be reported. These values serve for a smooth 
          transition when Efficiency is set to Murphree. 
          
      Murphree: Murphree vapor-phase efficiency will be applied.
          Murphree efficiency represents an efficiency model based on the concept of a 
          just partial approach to the vapor phase separation compared to the separation 
          if full equilibrium was applied to the vapor phase.
          Efficiency should not be set to Murphree when configuring the column 
          with Setup=Configure.
          The Murphree option will only be active in Process mode for design purposes.

In Fluid Flow and Dynamics modes only Contact efficiency will be applied.

name: Thermophysical
description: Column option to calculate thermophysical variables

name: RxnRateBasis
description:
Global fluid equilibrium reaction rate basis.
     Absolute: Reaction rates represent absolute molar rates of change. 
     Per Unit Feed: Reaction rates are normalized based on the feed flow 
         rate. Representing the molar change per unit feed, they become
         independent of the absolute flow rate (preferred).

Both options yield identical results, but require different formulations
in the reaction Rate model. “Per Unit Feed” is recommended due to
better numerical stability when stream flows approach zero rate.
Starting with v2023 fluid reactions are defaulted to the "Per Unit Feed"
option.  The "Absolute" option is retained for compatibility with
simulations built before v2023 and will be removed in a future release.

name: FeedVapor
description:
Column FeedVapor option. This option enables to either keep the feed streams' vapor contributions on 
the feed stage or to send their vapor contributions to the stage above.
     On Stage: the individual feed streams' vapor contributions are provided to the stage.
     Ramp: all the individual feed streams' vapor contributions are provided to the stage above per FVR[].
     Above Stage: the individual feed streams' vapor contributions are provided to the stage above.
Expand FVR in the Advanced Configuration group to set the each feed's fraction of vapor to be sent to the stage above, and 
expand FinV and FinL in the Profiles group to see the vapor and liquid feed to each stage.

name: PhasesCond
description:
Column option to override the fluid phase selection in the condenser
     Fluid: the FluidType's phases are applied
     VLE: The phases are set to VLE
     VLWE: The phases are set to VLWE
     VLLE: The phases are set to VLLE

name: PhasesBottom
description:
Column option to override the fluid phase selection in the reboiler and sump
     Fluid: the FluidType's phases are applied
     VLE: The phases are set to VLE
     VLWE: The phases are set to VLWE
     VLLE: The phases are set to VLLE
     VLLE Bulk: The phases are set to VLLE reporting only bulk liquid phase properties

name: SumpVaporReturn
description:
Column option to route reboiler return vapor
    Mix With Sump: reboiler return vapor mixes with sump liquid.
    To Stage Above: reboiler return vapor goes to stage above sump directly.
Mix With Sump option will be deprecated in the future. Please set SumpVaporReturn = To Stage Above.

name: ThermophysicalShow
description: Column fixed setting to calculate thermophysical variables (do not change)
